items.prompt: What shared benefit do SSL applications emphasize across vision, NLP, and time series?
choices: Data efficiency when downstream labels remain scarce
Eliminating all evaluation on labeled test sets
Replacing sensors with synthetic labels only
items.explain: Section 11.8 stresses unlabeled pretraining plus limited fine-tuning for data efficiency.

items.prompt: Self-supervised vision pretraining can reduce the labeled volume needed for competitive accuracy.
items.explain: Vision applications use unlabeled pretraining before scarce labels for detection or classification.

items.prompt: How can SSL support time-series anomaly detection?
choices: Train on normal unlabeled behavior and flag large prediction residuals
Require expert labels on every time step before any training
items.explain: Section 11.8 describes predicting next values and treating large deviations as anomalies.

items.prompt: Name one NLP benefit of pretrained language models with limited fine-tuning labels.
items.rubric: Fewer task labels / transfer of context / better downstream classification or NER.
items.sample_answer: Downstream tasks need far fewer task-specific labels than training from scratch.

items.prompt: Self-supervision removes the need to validate models on held-out gold labels.
items.explain: The part notes that teams still validate on held-out gold; SSL reduces early labeling demand.
